name: op_dpi_changed
description: ZOMBIE-OP PROOF (dpi_changed): `dpi_changed` was declared with no match arm — the 96 [dpi/*] corpus lines were therefore ungeneratable, and any test using it would have been vacuously green. It now sets FullWindowState.size.dpi through CallbackChange::ModifyWindowState; apply_user_change() saves previous_window_state, applies the scale, tags RelayoutReason::Resize + frame_needs_regeneration (what WM_DPICHANGED and the X11 DPI path do) and runs the state-diff pass, and the op asks for the relayout+repaint a real scale change forces. Proof that it is not a no-op: the DPI and hidpi factor really change, the LOGICAL size stays put while the PHYSICAL/backing size rescales with it (400x300 @96 -> 800x600 @192 -> 500x375 @120 — the framebuffer is genuinely reallocated at the new scale), the relayout really re-runs, and the DOM survives it. Against the old zombie every one of those goes red: the op answered `ok` and the DPI stayed 96. TWO deliberate omissions, so this scenario tests ONE thing and does not conflate bugs: (1) the window size is driven with an explicit `resize` op, not the `setup` block, because E2eSetup.window_width / window_height do not reach the headless window (it stays at the 640x480 default) — separate tracked bug, see e2e/bug-window-size-ignored.json. (2) there is NO assert_changed / assert_idle_stable here, because a surface-SIZE change currently reports FrameDamage::None: after a `dpi_changed` the pixels are re-rasterised at the new scale but the damage tracker reports nothing repainted. That is NOT specific to dpi_changed — a plain `resize` (a long-standing, non-zombie op) reports `damage: none` in exactly the same way, so it is a pre-existing gap in damage tracking across a buffer realloc and it needs its own bug-*.json scenario. Asserting it here would make this scenario red for a bug it is not about.

css: html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; }
